members.name: BufferClear
members.signature: public int BufferClear(RID buffer, int offset, int sizeBytes)
members.summary: Clears the contents of the [param buffer], clearing [param size_bytes] bytes, starting at [param offset]. Prints an error if: - the size isn't a multiple of four - the region specified by [param offset] + [param size_bytes] exceeds the buffer - a draw list is currently active (created by [method draw_list_begin]) - a compute list is currently active (created by [method compute_list_begin])

members.name: BufferCopy
members.signature: public int BufferCopy(RID srcBuffer, RID dstBuffer, int srcOffset, int dstOffset, int size)
members.summary: Copies [param size] bytes from the [param src_buffer] at [param src_offset] into [param dst_buffer] at [param dst_offset]. Prints an error if: - [param size] exceeds the size of either [param src_buffer] or [param dst_buffer] at their corresponding offsets - a draw list is currently active (created by [method draw_list_begin]) - a compute list is currently active (created by [method compute_list_begin])

members.name: BufferGetData
members.signature: public PackedByteArray BufferGetData(RID buffer, int offsetBytes, int sizeBytes)
members.summary: Returns a copy of the data of the specified [param buffer], optionally [param offset_bytes] and [param size_bytes] can be set to copy only a portion of the buffer. [b]Note:[/b] This method will block the GPU from working until the data is retrieved. Refer to [method buffer_get_data_async] for an alternative that returns the data in more performant way.

members.name: BufferGetDataAsync
members.signature: public int BufferGetDataAsync(RID buffer, Callable callback, int offsetBytes, int sizeBytes)
members.summary: Asynchronous version of [method buffer_get_data]. RenderingDevice will call [param callback] in a certain amount of frames with the data the buffer had at the time of the request. [b]Note:[/b] At the moment, the delay corresponds to the amount of frames specified by [member ProjectSettings.rendering/rendering_device/vsync/frame_queue_size]. [b]Note:[/b] Downloading large buffers can have a prohibitive cost for real-time even when using the asynchronous method due to hardware bandwidth limitations. When dealing with large resources, you can adjust settings such as [member ProjectSettings.rendering/rendering_device/staging_buffer/block_size_kb] to improve the transfer speed at the cost of extra memory. [codeblock] func _buffer_get_data_callback(array): value = array.decode_u32(0) ... rd.buffer_get_data_async(buffer, _buffer_get_data_callback) [/codeblock]

members.name: BufferGetDeviceAddress
members.signature: public int BufferGetDeviceAddress(RID buffer)
members.summary: Returns the address of the given [param buffer] which can be passed to shaders in any way to access underlying data. Buffer must have been created with this feature enabled. [b]Note:[/b] You must check that the GPU supports this functionality by calling [method has_feature] with [constant SUPPORTS_BUFFER_DEVICE_ADDRESS] as a parameter.

members.signature: public int BufferUpdate(RID buffer, int offset, int sizeBytes, PackedByteArray data)
members.summary: Updates a region of [param size_bytes] bytes, starting at [param offset], in the buffer, with the specified [param data]. Prints an error if: - the region specified by [param offset] + [param size_bytes] exceeds the buffer - a draw list is currently active (created by [method draw_list_begin]) - a compute list is currently active (created by [method compute_list_begin])

members.name: ComputeListBegin
members.signature: public int ComputeListBegin()
members.summary: Starts a list of compute commands created with the [code]compute_*[/code] methods. The returned value should be passed to other [code]compute_list_*[/code] functions. Multiple compute lists cannot be created at the same time; you must finish the previous compute list first using [method compute_list_end]. A simple compute operation might look like this (code is not a complete example): [codeblock] var rd = RenderingDevice.new() var compute_list = rd.compute_list_begin() rd.compute_list_bind_compute_pipeline(compute_list, compute_shader_dilate_pipeline) rd.compute_list_bind_uniform_set(compute_list, compute_base_uniform_set, 0) rd.compute_list_bind_uniform_set(compute_list, dilate_uniform_set, 1) for i in atlas_slices: rd.compute_list_set_push_constant(compute_list, push_constant, push_constant.size()) rd.compute_list_dispatch(compute_list, group_size.x, group_size.y, group_size.z) # No barrier, let them run all together. rd.compute_list_end() [/codeblock]

members.name: DrawCommandBeginLabel
members.signature: public void DrawCommandBeginLabel(String name, Color color)
members.summary: Create a command buffer debug label region that can be displayed in third-party tools such as [url=https://renderdoc.org/]RenderDoc[/url]. All regions must be ended with a [method draw_command_end_label] call. When viewed from the linear series of submissions to a single queue, calls to [method draw_command_begin_label] and [method draw_command_end_label] must be matched and balanced. The [code]VK_EXT_DEBUG_UTILS_EXTENSION_NAME[/code] Vulkan extension must be available and enabled for command buffer debug label region to work. See also [method draw_command_end_label].

members.name: DrawListBegin
members.signature: public int DrawListBegin(RID framebuffer, int drawFlags, PackedColorArray clearColorValues, float clearDepthValue, int clearStencilValue, Rect2 region, int breadcrumb)
members.summary: Starts a list of raster drawing commands created with the [code]draw_*[/code] methods. The returned value should be passed to other [code]draw_list_*[/code] functions. Multiple draw lists cannot be created at the same time; you must finish the previous draw list first using [method draw_list_end]. A simple drawing operation might look like this (code is not a complete example): [codeblock] var rd = RenderingDevice.new() var clear_colors = PackedColorArray([Color(0, 0, 0, 0), Color(0, 0, 0, 0), Color(0, 0, 0, 0)]) var draw_list = rd.draw_list_begin(framebuffers[i], RenderingDevice.CLEAR_COLOR_ALL, clear_colors, true, 1.0f, true, 0, Rect2(), RenderingDevice.OPAQUE_PASS) # Draw opaque. rd.draw_list_bind_render_pipeline(draw_list, raster_pipeline) rd.draw_list_bind_uniform_set(draw_list, raster_base_uniform, 0) rd.draw_list_set_push_constant(draw_list, raster_push_constant, raster_push_constant.size()) rd.draw_list_draw(draw_list, false, 1, slice_triangle_count[i] * 3) # Draw wire. rd.draw_list_bind_render_pipeline(draw_list, raster_pipeline_wire) rd.draw_list_bind_uniform_set(draw_list, raster_base_uniform, 0) rd.draw_list_set_push_constant(draw_list, raster_push_constant, raster_push_constant.size()) rd.draw_list_draw(draw_list, false, 1, slice_triangle_count[i] * 3) rd.draw_list_end() [/codeblock] The [param draw_flags] indicates if the texture attachments of the framebuffer should be cleared or ignored. Only one of the two flags can be used for each individual attachment. Ignoring an attachment means that any contents that existed before the draw list will be completely discarded, reducing the memory bandwidth used by the render pass but producing garbage results if the pixels aren't replaced. The default behavior allows the engine to figure out the right operation to use if the texture is discardable, which can result in increased performance. See [RDTextureFormat] or [method texture_set_discardable]. The [param breadcrumb] parameter can be an arbitrary 32-bit integer that is useful to diagnose GPU crashes. If Godot is built in dev or debug mode; when the GPU crashes Godot will dump all shaders that were being executed at the time of the crash and the breadcrumb is useful to diagnose what passes did those shaders belong to. It does not affect rendering behavior and can be set to 0. It is recommended to use [enum BreadcrumbMarker] enumerations for consistency but it's not required. It is also possible to use bitwise operations to add extra data. e.g. [codeblock] rd.draw_list_begin(fb[i], RenderingDevice.CLEAR_COLOR_ALL, clear_colors, true, 1.0f, true, 0, Rect2(), RenderingDevice.OPAQUE_PASS | 5) [/codeblock]

Scissor rectangles are used for clipping by discarding fragments that fall outside a specified rectangular portion of the screen. See also [method draw_list_disable_scissor]. [b]Note:[/b] The specified [param rect] is automatically intersected with the screen's dimensions, which means it cannot exceed the screen's dimensions.

This is only used by Vulkan in debug builds and can return 0 when this information is not tracked or unknown.

members.signature: public String GetDevicePipelineCacheUuid()
members.summary: Returns the universally unique identifier for the pipeline cache. This is used to cache shader files on disk, which avoids shader recompilations on subsequent engine runs. This UUID varies depending on the graphics card model, but also the driver version. Therefore, updating graphics drivers will invalidate the shader cache.

members.name: GetDriverAllocationCount

members.name: GetDriverAllocsByObjectType
members.signature: public int GetDriverAllocsByObjectType(int type)
members.summary: Same as [method get_driver_allocation_count] but filtered for a given object type. The type argument must be in range [code][0; get_tracked_object_type_count - 1][/code]. Godot must also be started with the [code]--extra-gpu-memory-tracking[/code] [url=$DOCS_URL/tutorials/editor/command_line_tutorial.html]command line argument[/url].

members.name: GetFrameDelay
members.signature: public int GetFrameDelay()
members.summary: Returns the frame count kept by the graphics API. Higher values result in higher input lag, but with more consistent throughput. For the main [RenderingDevice], frames are cycled (usually 3 with triple-buffered V-Sync enabled). However, local [RenderingDevice]s only have 1 frame.

members.name: GetMemoryUsage
members.signature: public int GetMemoryUsage(int type)
members.summary: Returns the memory usage in bytes corresponding to the given [param type]. When using Vulkan, these statistics are calculated by [url=https://github.com/GPUOpen-LibrariesAndSDKs/VulkanMemoryAllocator]Vulkan Memory Allocator[/url].

members.name: GetPerfReport
members.signature: public String GetPerfReport()
members.summary: Returns a string with a performance report from the past frame. Updates every frame.

members.name: GetTrackedObjectName
members.signature: public String GetTrackedObjectName(int typeIndex)
members.summary: Returns the name of the type of object for the given [param type_index]. This value must be in range [code][0; get_tracked_object_type_count - 1][/code]. If [method get_tracked_object_type_count] is 0, then type argument is ignored and always returns the same string. The return value is important because it gives meaning to the types passed to [method get_driver_memory_by_object_type], [method get_driver_allocs_by_object_type], [method get_device_memory_by_object_type], and [method get_device_allocs_by_object_type]. Examples of strings it can return (not exhaustive): - DEVICE_MEMORY - PIPELINE_CACHE - SWAPCHAIN_KHR - COMMAND_POOL Thus if e.g. [code]get_tracked_object_name(5)[/code] returns "COMMAND_POOL", then [code]get_device_memory_by_object_type(5)[/code] returns the bytes used by the GPU for command pools. This is only used by Vulkan in debug builds. Godot must also be started with the [code]--extra-gpu-memory-tracking[/code] [url=$DOCS_URL/tutorials/editor/command_line_tutorial.html]command line argument[/url].

members.name: GetTrackedObjectTypeCount
members.signature: public int GetTrackedObjectTypeCount()
members.summary: Returns how many types of trackable objects there are. This is only used by Vulkan in debug builds. Godot must also be started with the [code]--extra-gpu-memory-tracking[/code] [url=$DOCS_URL/tutorials/editor/command_line_tutorial.html]command line argument[/url].

members.name: HasFeature
members.signature: public bool HasFeature(int feature)
members.summary: Returns [code]true[/code] if the [param feature] is supported by the GPU.

members.name: HitSbtCreate
members.signature: public RID HitSbtCreate(RID raytracingPipeline, int initialHitGroupCapacity)
members.summary: Creates a new hit shader binding table (SBT). It can be accessed with the RID that is returned. Once finished with your RID, you will want to free the RID using the RenderingDevice's [method free_rid] method. This will be freed automatically when the [param raytracing_pipeline] is freed. The hit SBT resizes itself as needed. [param initial_hit_group_capacity] is used to allocate the initial backing memory.

members.name: HitSbtRangeAlloc
members.signature: public int HitSbtRangeAlloc(RID hitSbt, int hitGroupCount)
members.summary: Allocates a contiguous range of SBT entries from [param hit_sbt]. The returned value should be assigned to [member RDAccelerationStructureInstance.hit_sbt_range]. During ray traversal, hit group index is computed as: (geometry index in [member RDAccelerationStructureInstance.blas]) × (SBT stride used in [code]traceRayEXT[/code]) + (SBT offset used in [code]traceRayEXT[/code]) + (range offset) [param hit_group_count] must be large enough to cover all SBT entries that may be indexed by this equation. This typically corresponds to: (geometry count in [member RDAccelerationStructureInstance.blas]) × (SBT stride used in [code]traceRayEXT[/code]) The allocated range is uninitialized and must be filled using [method hit_sbt_range_update].

members.name: HitSbtRangeFree
members.signature: public int HitSbtRangeFree(RID hitSbt, int range)
members.summary: Frees a hit SBT range previously allocated with [method hit_sbt_range_alloc]. The range must not be in use by any acceleration structure after being freed.

members.name: HitSbtRangeUpdate
members.signature: public int HitSbtRangeUpdate(RID hitSbt, int range, int offset, PackedInt32Array hitGroupIndices)
members.summary: Updates the contents of a hit SBT range. [param hit_group_indices] specifies indices into the hit group array provided in [method raytracing_pipeline_create]. The [param offset] parameter specifies where within the allocated range the writing begins. This allows partial updates of a range. However, the complete range must be fully initialized before it is used in a raytracing dispatch.

members.name: HitSbtSetPipeline
members.signature: public int HitSbtSetPipeline(RID hitSbt, RID raytracingPipeline)
members.summary: Sets a new [param raytracing_pipeline] for [param hit_sbt]. The new pipeline must be a superset of the previous one. Existing hit groups must keep the same order and new hit groups should be appended to the end. This preserves existing SBT entries. The previous pipeline must remain valid during the call.

members.name: IndexArrayCreate
members.signature: public RID IndexArrayCreate(RID indexBuffer, int indexOffset, int indexCount)
members.summary: Creates a new index array. It can be accessed with the RID that is returned. Once finished with your RID, you will want to free the RID using the RenderingDevice's [method free_rid] method. This will be freed automatically when the [param index_buffer] is freed.

members.name: IndexBufferCreate
members.signature: public RID IndexBufferCreate(int sizeIndices, int format, PackedByteArray data, bool useRestartIndices, int creationBits)
members.summary: Creates a new index buffer. It can be accessed with the RID that is returned. Once finished with your RID, you will want to free the RID using the RenderingDevice's [method free_rid] method.

members.name: LimitGet
members.signature: public int LimitGet(int limit)
members.summary: Returns the value of the specified [param limit]. This limit varies depending on the current graphics hardware (and sometimes the driver version). If the given limit is exceeded, rendering errors will occur. Limits for various graphics hardware can be found in the [url=https://vulkan.gpuinfo.org/]Vulkan Hardware Database[/url].

members.name: RaytracingListBegin
members.signature: public int RaytracingListBegin()
members.summary: Starts a list of raytracing commands. The returned value should be passed to other [code]raytracing_list_*[/code] functions. Multiple raytracing lists cannot be created at the same time; you must finish the previous raytracing list first using [method raytracing_list_end]. A simple raytracing operation might look like this (code is not a complete example): [codeblocks] [gdscript] var rd = RenderingDevice.new() assert(rd.has_feature(RenderingDevice.SUPPORTS_RAYTRACING_PIPELINE)) # Create a BLAS for a mesh. var geometry = RDAccelerationStructureGeometry.new() geometry.flags = RenderingDevice.ACCELERATION_STRUCTURE_GEOMETRY_OPAQUE_BIT geometry.vertex_buffer = vertex_buffer geometry.vertex_stride = 12 geometry.vertex_format = RenderingDevice.DATA_FORMAT_R32G32B32_SFLOAT geometry.vertex_count = 3 geometry.index_buffer = index_buffer geometry.index_count = 3 geometries.push_back(geometry) blas = rd.blas_create([geometry], 0) # Create TLAS. tlas = rd.tlas_create(1, 0) # Build acceleration structures. rd.blas_build(blas) var instance = RDAccelerationStructureInstance.new() instance.blas = blas instance.hit_sbt_range = rd.hit_sbt_range_alloc(hit_sbt, 1) rd.hit_sbt_range_update(hit_sbt, instance.hit_sbt_range, 0, [0]) rd.tlas_build(tlas, [instance]) var raylist = rd.raytracing_list_begin() # Bind pipeline and uniforms. rd.raytracing_list_bind_raytracing_pipeline(raylist, raytracing_pipeline) rd.raytracing_list_bind_uniform_set(raylist, uniform_set, 0) # Trace rays. var width = get_viewport().size.x var height = get_viewport().size.y rd.raytracing_list_trace_rays(raylist, 0, hit_sbt, width, height, 1) rd.raytracing_list_end() [/gdscript] [/codeblocks]

members.name: RaytracingListBindRaytracingPipeline
members.signature: public void RaytracingListBindRaytracingPipeline(int raytracingList, RID raytracingPipeline)
members.summary: Binds [param raytracing_pipeline] to the specified [param raytracing_list].

members.name: RaytracingListBindUniformSet
members.signature: public void RaytracingListBindUniformSet(int raytracingList, RID uniformSet, int setIndex)
members.summary: Binds the [param uniform_set] to this [param raytracing_list].

members.name: RaytracingListEnd
members.signature: public void RaytracingListEnd()
members.summary: Finishes a list of raytracing commands created with the [code]raytracing_*[/code] methods.

members.name: RaytracingListSetPushConstant
members.signature: public void RaytracingListSetPushConstant(int raytracingList, PackedByteArray buffer, int sizeBytes)
members.summary: Sets the push constant data to [param buffer] for the specified [param raytracing_list]. The shader determines how this binary data is used. The buffer's size in bytes must also be specified in [param size_bytes] (this can be obtained by calling the [method PackedByteArray.size] method on the passed [param buffer]).

members.name: RaytracingListTraceRays
members.signature: public void RaytracingListTraceRays(int raytracingList, int raygenShaderIndex, RID hitSbt, int width, int height, int depth)
members.summary: Initializes a raytracing dispatch for [param raytracing_list], launching [param width] × [param height] × [param depth] rays. [param raygen_shader_index] selects the ray generation shader from the pipeline bound with [method raytracing_list_bind_raytracing_pipeline]. [param hit_sbt] must use the same pipeline bound to [param raytracing_list].

members.name: RaytracingPipelineCreate
members.signature: public RID RaytracingPipelineCreate(RDPipelineShader[] raygenShaders, RDPipelineShader[] missShaders, RDHitGroup[] hitGroups, int maxTraceRecursionDepth)
members.summary: Creates a new raytracing pipeline. It can be accessed with the RID that is returned. Once finished with your RID, you will want to free the RID using the RenderingDevice's [method free_rid] method. Each shader must provide the required stage. All stages must use compatible pipeline layouts. The pipeline selects the required stage from each shader. Input order defines stable indices used by the API: - [param raygen_shaders] is indexed in [method raytracing_list_trace_rays]. - [param miss_shaders] is indexed in [code]traceRayEXT[/code]. - [param hit_groups] is indexed in [method hit_sbt_range_update].

members.name: RaytracingPipelineIsValid
members.signature: public bool RaytracingPipelineIsValid(RID raytracingPipeline)
members.summary: Returns [code]true[/code] if the raytracing pipeline specified by the [param raytracing_pipeline] RID is valid, [code]false[/code] otherwise.

members.summary: Returns the framebuffer format of the given screen. [b]Note:[/b] Only the main [RenderingDevice] returned by [method RenderingServer.get_rendering_device] has a format. If called on a local [RenderingDevice], this method prints an error and returns [constant INVALID_ID].

members.name: ScreenGetHeight
members.signature: public int ScreenGetHeight(int screen)
members.summary: Returns the window height matching the graphics API context for the given window ID (in pixels). Despite the parameter being named [param screen], this returns the [i]window[/i] size. See also [method screen_get_width]. [b]Note:[/b] Only the main [RenderingDevice] returned by [method RenderingServer.get_rendering_device] has a height. If called on a local [RenderingDevice], this method prints an error and returns [constant INVALID_ID].

members.name: ScreenGetWidth
members.signature: public int ScreenGetWidth(int screen)
members.summary: Returns the window width matching the graphics API context for the given window ID (in pixels). Despite the parameter being named [param screen], this returns the [i]window[/i] size. See also [method screen_get_height]. [b]Note:[/b] Only the main [RenderingDevice] returned by [method RenderingServer.get_rendering_device] has a width. If called on a local [RenderingDevice], this method prints an error and returns [constant INVALID_ID].

members.name: SetResourceName
members.signature: public void SetResourceName(RID id, String name)
members.summary: Sets the resource name for [param id] to [param name]. This is used for debugging with third-party tools such as [url=https://renderdoc.org/]RenderDoc[/url]. The following types of resources can be named: texture, sampler, vertex buffer, index buffer, uniform buffer, texture buffer, storage buffer, uniform set buffer, shader, render pipeline and compute pipeline. Framebuffers cannot be named. Attempting to name an incompatible resource type will print an error. [b]Note:[/b] Resource names are only set when the engine runs in verbose mode ([method OS.is_stdout_verbose] = [code]true[/code]), or when using an engine build compiled with the [code]dev_mode=yes[/code] SCons option. The graphics driver must also support the [code]VK_EXT_DEBUG_UTILS_EXTENSION_NAME[/code] Vulkan extension for named resources to work.

members.name: ShaderCompileBinaryFromSpirv
members.signature: public PackedByteArray ShaderCompileBinaryFromSpirv(RDShaderSPIRV spirvData, String name)
members.summary: Compiles a binary shader from [param spirv_data] and returns the compiled binary data as a [PackedByteArray]. This compiled shader is specific to the GPU model and driver version used; it will not work on different GPU models or even different driver versions. See also [method shader_compile_spirv_from_source]. [param name] is an optional human-readable name that can be given to the compiled shader for organizational purposes.

members.name: ShaderCompileSpirvFromSource
members.signature: public RDShaderSPIRV ShaderCompileSpirvFromSource(RDShaderSource shaderSource, bool allowCache)
members.summary: Compiles a SPIR-V from the shader source code in [param shader_source] and returns the SPIR-V as an [RDShaderSPIRV]. This intermediate language shader is portable across different GPU models and driver versions, but cannot be run directly by GPUs until compiled into a binary shader using [method shader_compile_binary_from_spirv]. If [param allow_cache] is [code]true[/code], make use of the shader cache generated by Godot. This avoids a potentially lengthy shader compilation step if the shader is already in cache. If [param allow_cache] is [code]false[/code], Godot's shader cache is ignored and the shader will always be recompiled.

members.name: ShaderCreateFromBytecode
members.signature: public RID ShaderCreateFromBytecode(PackedByteArray binaryData, RID placeholderRid)
members.summary: Creates a new shader instance from a binary compiled shader. It can be accessed with the RID that is returned. Once finished with your RID, you will want to free the RID using the RenderingDevice's [method free_rid] method. See also [method shader_compile_binary_from_spirv] and [method shader_create_from_spirv].

members.name: ShaderCreateFromSpirv
members.signature: public RID ShaderCreateFromSpirv(RDShaderSPIRV spirvData, String name)
members.summary: Creates a new shader instance from SPIR-V intermediate code. It can be accessed with the RID that is returned. Once finished with your RID, you will want to free the RID using the RenderingDevice's [method free_rid] method. See also [method shader_compile_spirv_from_source] and [method shader_create_from_bytecode].

members.name: ShaderCreatePlaceholder
members.signature: public RID ShaderCreatePlaceholder()
members.summary: Create a placeholder RID by allocating an RID without initializing it for use in [method shader_create_from_bytecode]. This allows you to create an RID for a shader and pass it around, but defer compiling the shader to a later time.

members.name: ShaderGetVertexInputAttributeMask
members.signature: public int ShaderGetVertexInputAttributeMask(RID shader)
members.summary: Returns the internal vertex input mask. Internally, the vertex input mask is an unsigned integer consisting of the locations (specified in GLSL via. [code]layout(location = ...)[/code]) of the input variables (specified in GLSL by the [code]in[/code] keyword).

members.name: StorageBufferCreate
members.signature: public RID StorageBufferCreate(int sizeBytes, PackedByteArray data, int usage, int creationBits)
members.summary: Creates a [url=https://vkguide.dev/docs/chapter-4/storage_buffers/]storage buffer[/url] with the specified [param data] and [param usage]. It can be accessed with the RID that is returned. Once finished with your RID, you will want to free the RID using the RenderingDevice's [method free_rid] method.

members.name: Submit
members.signature: public void Submit()
members.summary: Pushes the frame setup and draw command buffers then marks the local device as currently processing (which allows calling [method sync]). [b]Note:[/b] Only available in local RenderingDevices.

members.name: Sync
members.signature: public void Sync()
members.summary: Forces a synchronization between the CPU and GPU, which may be required in certain cases. Only call this when needed, as CPU-GPU synchronization has a performance cost. [b]Note:[/b] Only available in local RenderingDevices. [b]Note:[/b] [method sync] can only be called after a [method submit].

members.name: TextureBufferCreate
members.signature: public RID TextureBufferCreate(int sizeBytes, int format, PackedByteArray data)
members.summary: Creates a new texture buffer. It can be accessed with the RID that is returned. Once finished with your RID, you will want to free the RID using the RenderingDevice's [method free_rid] method.

members.name: TextureClear
members.signature: public int TextureClear(RID texture, Color color, int baseMipmap, int mipmapCount, int baseLayer, int layerCount)
members.summary: Clears the specified [param texture] by replacing all of its pixels with the specified [param color]. [param base_mipmap] and [param mipmap_count] determine which mipmaps of the texture are affected by this clear operation, while [param base_layer] and [param layer_count] determine which layers of a 3D texture (or texture array) are affected by this clear operation. For 2D textures (which only have one layer by design), [param base_layer] must be [code]0[/code] and [param layer_count] must be [code]1[/code]. [b]Note:[/b] [param texture] can't be cleared while a draw list that uses it as part of a framebuffer is being created. Ensure the draw list is finalized (and that the color/depth texture using it is not set to [constant FINAL_ACTION_CONTINUE]) to clear this texture.

members.name: TextureCopy
members.signature: public int TextureCopy(RID fromTexture, RID toTexture, Vector3 fromPos, Vector3 toPos, Vector3 size, int srcMipmap, int dstMipmap, int srcLayer, int dstLayer)
members.summary: Copies the [param from_texture] to [param to_texture] with the specified [param from_pos], [param to_pos] and [param size] coordinates. For 2-dimensional textures, [param from_pos] and [param to_pos] must have a Z axis of [code]0[/code], and [param size] must have a Z axis of [code]1[/code]. Source and destination mipmaps/layers must also be specified, with these parameters being [code]0[/code] for textures without mipmaps or single-layer textures. Returns [constant @GlobalScope.OK] if the texture copy was successful or [constant @GlobalScope.ERR_INVALID_PARAMETER] otherwise. [b]Note:[/b] [param from_texture] texture can't be copied while a draw list that uses it as part of a framebuffer is being created. Ensure the draw list is finalized (and that the color/depth texture using it is not set to [constant FINAL_ACTION_CONTINUE]) to copy this texture. [b]Note:[/b] [param from_texture] texture requires the [constant TEXTURE_USAGE_CAN_COPY_FROM_BIT] to be retrieved. [b]Note:[/b] [param to_texture] can't be copied while a draw list that uses it as part of a framebuffer is being created. Ensure the draw list is finalized (and that the color/depth texture using it is not set to [constant FINAL_ACTION_CONTINUE]) to copy this texture. [b]Note:[/b] [param to_texture] requires the [constant TEXTURE_USAGE_CAN_COPY_TO_BIT] to be retrieved. [b]Note:[/b] [param from_texture] and [param to_texture] must be of the same type (color or depth).

members.name: TextureCreate
members.signature: public RID TextureCreate(RDTextureFormat format, RDTextureView view, PackedByteArray[] data)
members.summary: Creates a new texture. It can be accessed with the RID that is returned. Once finished with your RID, you will want to free the RID using the RenderingDevice's [method free_rid] method. [b]Note:[/b] [param data] takes an [Array] of [PackedByteArray]s. For [constant TEXTURE_TYPE_1D], [constant TEXTURE_TYPE_2D], and [constant TEXTURE_TYPE_3D] types, this array should only have one element, a [PackedByteArray] containing all the data for the texture. For [code]_ARRAY[/code] and [code]_CUBE[/code] types, the length should be the same as the number of [member RDTextureFormat.array_layers] in [param format]. [b]Note:[/b] Not to be confused with [method RenderingServer.texture_2d_create], which creates the Godot-specific [Texture2D] resource as opposed to the graphics API's own texture type.

members.name: TextureCreateFromExtension
members.signature: public RID TextureCreateFromExtension(int type, int format, int samples, int usageFlags, int image, int width, int height, int depth, int layers, int mipmaps)
members.summary: Returns an RID for an existing [param image] ([code]VkImage[/code]) with the given [param type], [param format], [param samples], [param usage_flags], [param width], [param height], [param depth], [param layers], and [param mipmaps]. This can be used to allow Godot to render onto foreign images.

members.name: TextureCreateShared
members.signature: public RID TextureCreateShared(RDTextureView view, RID withTexture)
members.summary: Creates a shared texture using the specified [param view] and the texture information from [param with_texture]. This will be freed automatically when the [param with_texture] is freed.

members.name: TextureCreateSharedFromSlice
members.signature: public RID TextureCreateSharedFromSlice(RDTextureView view, RID withTexture, int layer, int mipmap, int mipmaps, int sliceType)
members.summary: Creates a shared texture using the specified [param view] and the texture information from [param with_texture]'s [param layer] and [param mipmap]. The number of included mipmaps from the original texture can be controlled using the [param mipmaps] parameter. Only relevant for textures with multiple layers, such as 3D textures, texture arrays and cubemaps. For single-layer textures, use [method texture_create_shared]. For 2D textures (which only have one layer), [param layer] must be [code]0[/code]. [b]Note:[/b] Layer slicing is only supported for 2D texture arrays, not 3D textures or cubemaps. This will be freed automatically when the [param with_texture] is freed.

members.name: TextureGetData
members.signature: public PackedByteArray TextureGetData(RID texture, int layer)
members.summary: Returns the [param texture] data for the specified [param layer] as raw binary data. For 2D textures (which only have one layer), [param layer] must be [code]0[/code]. [b]Note:[/b] [param texture] can't be retrieved while a draw list that uses it as part of a framebuffer is being created. Ensure the draw list is finalized (and that the color/depth texture using it is not set to [constant FINAL_ACTION_CONTINUE]) to retrieve this texture. Otherwise, an error is printed and an empty [PackedByteArray] is returned. [b]Note:[/b] [param texture] requires the [constant TEXTURE_USAGE_CAN_COPY_FROM_BIT] to be retrieved. Otherwise, an error is printed and an empty [PackedByteArray] is returned. [b]Note:[/b] This method will block the GPU from working until the data is retrieved. Refer to [method texture_get_data_async] for an alternative that returns the data in more performant way.

members.name: TextureGetDataAsync
members.signature: public int TextureGetDataAsync(RID texture, int layer, Callable callback)
members.summary: Asynchronous version of [method texture_get_data]. RenderingDevice will call [param callback] in a certain amount of frames with the data the texture had at the time of the request. [b]Note:[/b] At the moment, the delay corresponds to the amount of frames specified by [member ProjectSettings.rendering/rendering_device/vsync/frame_queue_size]. [b]Note:[/b] Downloading large textures can have a prohibitive cost for real-time even when using the asynchronous method due to hardware bandwidth limitations. When dealing with large resources, you can adjust settings such as [member ProjectSettings.rendering/rendering_device/staging_buffer/texture_download_region_size_px] and [member ProjectSettings.rendering/rendering_device/staging_buffer/block_size_kb] to improve the transfer speed at the cost of extra memory. [codeblock] func _texture_get_data_callback(array): value = array.decode_u32(0) ... rd.texture_get_data_async(texture, 0, _texture_get_data_callback) [/codeblock]

members.name: TextureGetFormat

members.summary: Returns the data format used to create this texture.

members.name: TextureGetNativeHandle
members.signature: public int TextureGetNativeHandle(RID texture)
members.summary: Returns the internal graphics handle for this texture object. For use when communicating with third-party APIs mostly with GDExtension. [b]Note:[/b] This function returns a [code]uint64_t[/code] which internally maps to a [code]GLuint[/code] (OpenGL) or [code]VkImage[/code] (Vulkan).

members.name: TextureIsDiscardable
members.signature: public bool TextureIsDiscardable(RID texture)
members.summary: Returns [code]true[/code] if the [param texture] is discardable, [code]false[/code] otherwise. See [RDTextureFormat] or [method texture_set_discardable].

members.name: TextureIsFormatSupportedForUsage
members.signature: public bool TextureIsFormatSupportedForUsage(int format, int usageFlags)
members.summary: Returns [code]true[/code] if the specified [param format] is supported for the given [param usage_flags], [code]false[/code] otherwise.

members.name: TextureIsShared
members.signature: public bool TextureIsShared(RID texture)
members.summary: Returns [code]true[/code] if the [param texture] is shared, [code]false[/code] otherwise. See [RDTextureView].

members.name: TextureIsValid
members.signature: public bool TextureIsValid(RID texture)
members.summary: Returns [code]true[/code] if the [param texture] is valid, [code]false[/code] otherwise.

members.name: TextureResolveMultisample
members.signature: public int TextureResolveMultisample(RID fromTexture, RID toTexture)
members.summary: Resolves the [param from_texture] texture onto [param to_texture] with multisample antialiasing enabled. This must be used when rendering a framebuffer for MSAA to work. Returns [constant @GlobalScope.OK] if successful, [constant @GlobalScope.ERR_INVALID_PARAMETER] otherwise. [b]Note:[/b] [param from_texture] and [param to_texture] textures must have the same dimension, format and type (color or depth). [b]Note:[/b] [param from_texture] can't be copied while a draw list that uses it as part of a framebuffer is being created. Ensure the draw list is finalized (and that the color/depth texture using it is not set to [constant FINAL_ACTION_CONTINUE]) to resolve this texture. [b]Note:[/b] [param from_texture] requires the [constant TEXTURE_USAGE_CAN_COPY_FROM_BIT] to be retrieved. [b]Note:[/b] [param from_texture] must be multisampled and must also be 2D (or a slice of a 3D/cubemap texture). [b]Note:[/b] [param to_texture] can't be copied while a draw list that uses it as part of a framebuffer is being created. Ensure the draw list is finalized (and that the color/depth texture using it is not set to [constant FINAL_ACTION_CONTINUE]) to resolve this texture. [b]Note:[/b] [param to_texture] texture requires the [constant TEXTURE_USAGE_CAN_COPY_TO_BIT] to be retrieved. [b]Note:[/b] [param to_texture] texture must [b]not[/b] be multisampled and must also be 2D (or a slice of a 3D/cubemap texture).

members.name: TextureSetDiscardable
members.signature: public void TextureSetDiscardable(RID texture, bool discardable)
members.summary: Updates the discardable property of [param texture]. If a texture is discardable, its contents do not need to be preserved between frames. This flag is only relevant when the texture is used as target in a draw list. This information is used by [RenderingDevice] to figure out if a texture's contents can be discarded, eliminating unnecessary writes to memory and boosting performance.

members.name: TextureUpdate
members.signature: public int TextureUpdate(RID texture, int layer, PackedByteArray data)
members.summary: Updates texture data with new data, replacing the previous data in place. The updated texture data must have the same dimensions and format. For 2D textures (which only have one layer), [param layer] must be [code]0[/code]. Returns [constant @GlobalScope.OK] if the update was successful, [constant @GlobalScope.ERR_INVALID_PARAMETER] otherwise. [b]Note:[/b] Updating textures is forbidden during creation of a draw or compute list. [b]Note:[/b] The existing [param texture] can't be updated while a draw list that uses it as part of a framebuffer is being created. Ensure the draw list is finalized (and that the color/depth texture using it is not set to [constant FINAL_ACTION_CONTINUE]) to update this texture. [b]Note:[/b] The existing [param texture] requires the [constant TEXTURE_USAGE_CAN_UPDATE_BIT] to be updatable.

members.name: TlasBuild
members.signature: public int TlasBuild(RID tlas, RDAccelerationStructureInstance[] instances)
members.summary: Builds the [param tlas]. The contents of previous builds are discarded. Any BLAS provided through the [member RDAccelerationStructureInstance.blas] member must already have been built using the [method blas_build] method. The number of instances can be equal to or smaller than the maximum instance count provided in the [method tlas_create] method. [b]Note:[/b] Freeing or rebuilding any of the provided BLASes after this method invalidates the TLAS and requires it to be rebuilt.

members.name: TlasCreate
members.signature: public RID TlasCreate(int maxInstanceCount, int flags)
members.summary: Creates a new Top-Level Acceleration Structure (TLAS). It can be accessed with the RID that is returned. Once finished with your RID, you will want to free the RID using the RenderingDevice's [method free_rid] method.

members.name: UniformBufferCreate
members.signature: public RID UniformBufferCreate(int sizeBytes, PackedByteArray data, int creationBits)
members.summary: Creates a new uniform buffer. It can be accessed with the RID that is returned. Once finished with your RID, you will want to free the RID using the RenderingDevice's [method free_rid] method.

members.name: UniformSetCreate
members.signature: public RID UniformSetCreate(RDUniform[] uniforms, RID shader, int shaderSet)
members.summary: Creates a new uniform set. It can be accessed with the RID that is returned. Once finished with your RID, you will want to free the RID using the RenderingDevice's [method free_rid] method. This will be freed automatically when the [param shader] or any of the RIDs in the [param uniforms] is freed.

members.name: UniformSetIsValid
members.signature: public bool UniformSetIsValid(RID uniformSet)
members.summary: Checks if the [param uniform_set] is valid, i.e. is owned.

members.name: VertexArrayCreate
members.signature: public RID VertexArrayCreate(int vertexCount, int vertexFormat, RID[] srcBuffers, PackedInt64Array offsets)
members.summary: Creates a vertex array based on the specified buffers. Optionally, [param offsets] (in bytes) may be defined for each buffer. Once finished with your RID, you will want to free the RID using the RenderingDevice's [method free_rid] method. This will be freed automatically when any of the [param src_buffers] is freed.

members.name: VertexBufferCreate
members.signature: public RID VertexBufferCreate(int sizeBytes, PackedByteArray data, int creationBits)
members.summary: Creates a new vertex buffer. It can be accessed with the RID that is returned. Once finished with your RID, you will want to free the RID using the RenderingDevice's [method free_rid] method.

members.name: VertexFormatCreate
members.signature: public int VertexFormatCreate(RDVertexAttribute[] vertexDescriptions)
members.summary: Creates a new vertex format with the specified [param vertex_descriptions]. Returns a unique vertex format ID corresponding to the newly created vertex format.

values.name: ACCELERATION_STRUCTURE_ALLOW_COMPACTION_BIT
values.summary: Allows the acceleration structure to be compacted to reduce memory usage after it has been built.

values.name: ACCELERATION_STRUCTURE_ALLOW_UPDATE_BIT
values.summary: Allows the acceleration structure to be updated after it has been built.

values.name: ACCELERATION_STRUCTURE_LOW_MEMORY_BIT
values.summary: Reduces the memory usage of the acceleration structure, potentially at the cost of reduced ray traversal performance.

values.name: ACCELERATION_STRUCTURE_PREFER_FAST_BUILD_BIT
values.summary: Prioritizes build performance over ray traversal performance when building the acceleration structure.

values.name: ACCELERATION_STRUCTURE_PREFER_FAST_TRACE_BIT
values.summary: Prioritizes ray traversal performance over build performance when building the acceleration structure.

values.name: ACCELERATION_STRUCTURE_GEOMETRY_NO_DUPLICATE_ANY_HIT_INVOCATION_BIT
values.summary: This geometry only calls the any hit shader a single time for each primitive.

values.name: ACCELERATION_STRUCTURE_GEOMETRY_OPAQUE_BIT
values.summary: An opaque geometry does not invoke the any hit shaders.

values.name: ACCELERATION_STRUCTURE_INSTANCE_FORCE_NO_OPAQUE_BIT
values.summary: Forces all geometries in this instance to be treated as non-opaque, allowing any hit shaders to be invoked.

values.name: ACCELERATION_STRUCTURE_INSTANCE_FORCE_OPAQUE_BIT
values.summary: Forces all geometries in this instance to be treated as opaque, preventing any hit shaders from being invoked.

values.name: ACCELERATION_STRUCTURE_INSTANCE_TRIANGLE_FACING_CULL_DISABLE_BIT
values.summary: Disables triangle face culling for this instance during ray traversal.

values.name: ACCELERATION_STRUCTURE_INSTANCE_TRIANGLE_FLIP_FACING_BIT
values.summary: Flips the triangle facing direction for this instance during ray traversal.

values.name: BUFFER_CREATION_ACCELERATION_STRUCTURE_BUILD_INPUT_READ_ONLY_BIT
values.summary: Allows usage of this buffer as input data for an acceleration structure build operation. You must first check that the GPU supports it: [codeblocks] [gdscript] rd = RenderingServer.get_rendering_device() if rd.has_feature(RenderingDevice.SUPPORTS_RAYTRACING_PIPELINE): storage_buffer = rd.storage_buffer_create(bytes.size(), bytes, RenderingDevice.BUFFER_CREATION_ACCELERATION_STRUCTURE_BUILD_INPUT_READ_ONLY_BIT) [/gdscript] [/codeblocks]

values.name: BUFFER_CREATION_AS_STORAGE_BIT
values.summary: Set this flag so that it is created as storage. This is useful if Compute Shaders need access (for reading or writing) to the buffer, e.g. skeletal animations are processed in Compute Shaders which need access to vertex buffers, to be later consumed by vertex shaders as part of the regular rasterization pipeline.

values.name: BUFFER_CREATION_DEVICE_ADDRESS_BIT
values.summary: Optionally, set this flag if you wish to use [method buffer_get_device_address] functionality. You must first check the GPU supports it: [codeblocks] [gdscript] rd = RenderingServer.get_rendering_device() if rd.has_feature(RenderingDevice.SUPPORTS_BUFFER_DEVICE_ADDRESS): storage_buffer = rd.storage_buffer_create(bytes.size(), bytes, RenderingDevice.STORAGE_BUFFER_USAGE_SHADER_DEVICE_ADDRESS) storage_buffer_address = rd.buffer_get_device_address(storage_buffer) [/gdscript] [/codeblocks]

values.name: DEVICE_TYPE_CPU
values.summary: Rendering device is provided by software emulation (such as Lavapipe or [url=https://github.com/google/swiftshader]SwiftShader[/url]). This is the slowest kind of rendering device available; it's typically much slower than [constant DEVICE_TYPE_INTEGRATED_GPU].

values.name: DEVICE_TYPE_DISCRETE_GPU
values.summary: Rendering device is a dedicated GPU, which is typically [i](but not always)[/i] faster than integrated GPUs ([constant DEVICE_TYPE_INTEGRATED_GPU]).

values.name: DEVICE_TYPE_INTEGRATED_GPU
values.summary: Rendering device is an integrated GPU, which is typically [i](but not always)[/i] slower than dedicated GPUs ([constant DEVICE_TYPE_DISCRETE_GPU]). On Android and iOS, the rendering device type is always considered to be [constant DEVICE_TYPE_INTEGRATED_GPU].

values.name: DEVICE_TYPE_MAX
values.summary: Represents the size of the [enum DeviceType] enum.

values.name: DEVICE_TYPE_OTHER
values.summary: Rendering device type does not match any of the other enum values or is unknown.

values.name: DEVICE_TYPE_VIRTUAL_GPU
values.summary: Rendering device is an emulated GPU in a virtual environment. This is typically much slower than the host GPU, which means the expected performance level on a dedicated GPU will be roughly equivalent to [constant DEVICE_TYPE_INTEGRATED_GPU]. Virtual machine GPU passthrough (such as VFIO) will not report the device type as [constant DEVICE_TYPE_VIRTUAL_GPU]. Instead, the host GPU's device type will be reported as if the GPU was not emulated.

values.name: DRIVER_RESOURCE_UNIFORM_SET
values.summary: - Vulkan: [code]VkDescriptorSet[/code].

values.name: SUPPORTS_BUFFER_DEVICE_ADDRESS
values.summary: Features support for buffer device address extension.

values.name: SUPPORTS_HDR_OUTPUT
values.summary: Support for high dynamic range (HDR) output.

values.name: SUPPORTS_IMAGE_ATOMIC_32_BIT
values.summary: Support for 32-bit image atomic operations.

values.name: SUPPORTS_METALFX_SPATIAL
values.summary: Support for MetalFX spatial upscaling.

values.name: SUPPORTS_METALFX_TEMPORAL
values.summary: Support for MetalFX temporal upscaling.

values.name: SUPPORTS_RAYTRACING_PIPELINE
values.summary: Support for raytracing pipeline extension. [b]Note:[/b] This is currently only supported when using Vulkan. This is not supported on macOS and iOS (even on hardware supporting raytracing) due to MoltenVK limitations.

values.name: SUPPORTS_RAY_QUERY
values.summary: Support for ray query extension. [b]Note:[/b] This is currently only supported when using Vulkan. This is not supported on macOS and iOS (even on hardware supporting raytracing) due to MoltenVK limitations.

values.name: FINAL_ACTION_DISCARD
values.summary: Discard the contents of the framebuffer. This is the fastest option if you don't need to use the results of the draw list.

values.name: FINAL_ACTION_MAX
values.summary: Represents the size of the [enum FinalAction] enum.

values.name: FINAL_ACTION_STORE
values.summary: Store the result of the draw list in the framebuffer. This is generally what you want to do.

values.name: INDEX_BUFFER_FORMAT_UINT16
values.summary: Index buffer in 16-bit unsigned integer format. This limits the maximum index that can be specified to [code]65535[/code].

values.name: INDEX_BUFFER_FORMAT_UINT32
values.summary: Index buffer in 32-bit unsigned integer format. This limits the maximum index that can be specified to [code]4294967295[/code].

values.name: INITIAL_ACTION_CLEAR
values.summary: Clear the whole framebuffer or its specified region.

values.name: INITIAL_ACTION_DISCARD
values.summary: Ignore the previous contents of the framebuffer. This is the fastest option if you'll overwrite all of the pixels and don't need to read any of them.

values.name: INITIAL_ACTION_LOAD
values.summary: Load the previous contents of the framebuffer.

values.name: INITIAL_ACTION_MAX
values.summary: Represents the size of the [enum InitialAction] enum.

values.name: LIMIT_MAX_BOUND_UNIFORM_SETS
values.summary: Maximum number of uniform sets that can be bound at a given time.

values.name: LIMIT_MAX_COMPUTE_SHARED_MEMORY_SIZE
values.summary: Maximum shared memory size for compute shaders.

values.name: LIMIT_MAX_COMPUTE_WORKGROUP_COUNT_X
values.summary: Maximum number of workgroups for compute shaders on the X axis.

values.name: LIMIT_MAX_COMPUTE_WORKGROUP_COUNT_Y
values.summary: Maximum number of workgroups for compute shaders on the Y axis.

values.name: LIMIT_MAX_COMPUTE_WORKGROUP_COUNT_Z
values.summary: Maximum number of workgroups for compute shaders on the Z axis.

values.name: LIMIT_MAX_COMPUTE_WORKGROUP_INVOCATIONS
values.summary: Maximum number of workgroup invocations for compute shaders.

values.name: LIMIT_MAX_COMPUTE_WORKGROUP_SIZE_X
values.summary: Maximum workgroup size for compute shaders on the X axis.

values.name: LIMIT_MAX_COMPUTE_WORKGROUP_SIZE_Y
values.summary: Maximum workgroup size for compute shaders on the Y axis.

values.name: LIMIT_MAX_COMPUTE_WORKGROUP_SIZE_Z
values.summary: Maximum workgroup size for compute shaders on the Z axis.

values.name: LIMIT_MAX_DRAW_INDEXED_INDEX
values.summary: Maximum index for an indexed draw command.

values.name: LIMIT_MAX_FRAMEBUFFER_COLOR_ATTACHMENTS
values.summary: Maximum number of color framebuffer attachments that can be used at a given time.

values.name: LIMIT_MAX_FRAMEBUFFER_HEIGHT
values.summary: Maximum height of a framebuffer (in pixels).

values.name: LIMIT_MAX_FRAMEBUFFER_WIDTH
values.summary: Maximum width of a framebuffer (in pixels).

values.name: LIMIT_MAX_PUSH_CONSTANT_SIZE
values.summary: Maximum size of a push constant. A lot of devices are limited to 128 bytes, so try to avoid exceeding 128 bytes in push constants to ensure compatibility even if your GPU is reporting a higher value.

values.name: LIMIT_MAX_SAMPLERS_PER_SHADER_STAGE
values.summary: Maximum number of samplers per shader stage.

values.name: LIMIT_MAX_SAMPLERS_PER_UNIFORM_SET
values.summary: Maximum number of samplers that can be used per uniform set.

values.name: LIMIT_MAX_STORAGE_BUFFERS_PER_SHADER_STAGE
values.summary: Maximum number of [url=https://vkguide.dev/docs/chapter-4/storage_buffers/]storage buffers[/url] per shader stage.

values.name: LIMIT_MAX_STORAGE_BUFFERS_PER_UNIFORM_SET
values.summary: Maximum number of [url=https://vkguide.dev/docs/chapter-4/storage_buffers/]storage buffers[/url] per uniform set.

values.name: LIMIT_MAX_STORAGE_IMAGES_PER_SHADER_STAGE
values.summary: Maximum number of storage images per shader stage.

values.name: LIMIT_MAX_STORAGE_IMAGES_PER_UNIFORM_SET
values.summary: Maximum number of storage images per uniform set.

values.name: LIMIT_MAX_TEXTURES_PER_SHADER_STAGE
values.summary: Maximum number of textures per shader stage.

values.name: LIMIT_MAX_TEXTURES_PER_UNIFORM_SET
values.summary: Maximum number of textures that can be used per uniform set.

values.name: LIMIT_MAX_TEXTURE_ARRAY_LAYERS
values.summary: Maximum number of texture array layers.

values.name: LIMIT_MAX_TEXTURE_SIZE_1D
values.summary: Maximum supported 1-dimensional texture size (in pixels on a single axis).

values.name: LIMIT_MAX_TEXTURE_SIZE_2D
values.summary: Maximum supported 2-dimensional texture size (in pixels on a single axis).

values.name: LIMIT_MAX_TEXTURE_SIZE_3D
values.summary: Maximum supported 3-dimensional texture size (in pixels on a single axis).

values.name: LIMIT_MAX_TEXTURE_SIZE_CUBE
values.summary: Maximum supported cubemap texture size (in pixels on a single axis of a single face).

values.name: LIMIT_MAX_UNIFORM_BUFFERS_PER_SHADER_STAGE
values.summary: Maximum number of uniform buffers per uniform set.

values.name: LIMIT_MAX_UNIFORM_BUFFERS_PER_UNIFORM_SET
values.summary: Maximum number of uniform buffers per uniform set.

values.name: LIMIT_MAX_UNIFORM_BUFFER_SIZE
values.summary: Maximum size of a uniform buffer.

values.name: LIMIT_MAX_VERTEX_INPUT_ATTRIBUTES
values.summary: Maximum number of vertex input attributes.

values.name: LIMIT_MAX_VERTEX_INPUT_ATTRIBUTE_OFFSET
values.summary: Maximum vertex input attribute offset.

values.name: LIMIT_MAX_VERTEX_INPUT_BINDINGS
values.summary: Maximum number of vertex input bindings.

values.name: LIMIT_MAX_VERTEX_INPUT_BINDING_STRIDE
values.summary: Maximum vertex input binding stride.

values.name: LIMIT_MAX_VIEWPORT_DIMENSIONS_X
values.summary: Maximum viewport width (in pixels).

values.name: LIMIT_MAX_VIEWPORT_DIMENSIONS_Y
values.summary: Maximum viewport height (in pixels).

values.name: LIMIT_METALFX_TEMPORAL_SCALER_MAX_SCALE
values.summary: Returns the largest value for [member ProjectSettings.rendering/scaling_3d/scale] when using the MetalFX temporal upscaler. [b]Note:[/b] The returned value is multiplied by a factor of [code]1000000[/code] to preserve 6 digits of precision. It must be divided by [code]1000000.0[/code] to convert the value to a floating point number.

values.name: LIMIT_METALFX_TEMPORAL_SCALER_MIN_SCALE
values.summary: Returns the smallest value for [member ProjectSettings.rendering/scaling_3d/scale] when using the MetalFX temporal upscaler. [b]Note:[/b] The returned value is multiplied by a factor of [code]1000000[/code] to preserve 6 digits of precision. It must be divided by [code]1000000.0[/code] to convert the value to a floating point number.

values.name: LIMIT_MIN_UNIFORM_BUFFER_OFFSET_ALIGNMENT
values.summary: Minimum uniform buffer offset alignment.

values.name: LOGIC_OP_AND
values.summary: AND logic operation.

values.name: LOGIC_OP_AND_INVERTED
values.summary: AND logic operation with the [i]source[/i] operand being inverted. See also [constant LOGIC_OP_AND_REVERSE].

values.name: LOGIC_OP_AND_REVERSE
values.summary: AND logic operation with the [i]destination[/i] operand being inverted. See also [constant LOGIC_OP_AND_INVERTED].

values.name: LOGIC_OP_CLEAR
values.summary: Clear logic operation (result is always [code]0[/code]). See also [constant LOGIC_OP_SET].

values.name: LOGIC_OP_COPY
values.summary: Copy logic operation (keeps the [i]source[/i] value as-is). See also [constant LOGIC_OP_COPY_INVERTED] and [constant LOGIC_OP_NO_OP].

values.name: LOGIC_OP_COPY_INVERTED
values.summary: NOT logic operation (inverts the value). See also [constant LOGIC_OP_COPY].

values.name: LOGIC_OP_EQUIVALENT
values.summary: Not-XOR (XNOR) logic operation.

values.name: LOGIC_OP_INVERT
values.summary: Invert logic operation.

values.name: LOGIC_OP_MAX
values.summary: Represents the size of the [enum LogicOperation] enum.

values.name: LOGIC_OP_NAND
values.summary: Not-AND (NAND) logic operation.

values.name: LOGIC_OP_NOR
values.summary: Not-OR (NOR) logic operation.

values.name: LOGIC_OP_NO_OP
values.summary: No-op logic operation (keeps the [i]destination[/i] value as-is). See also [constant LOGIC_OP_COPY].

values.name: LOGIC_OP_OR
values.summary: OR logic operation.

values.name: LOGIC_OP_OR_INVERTED
values.summary: OR logic operation with the [i]source[/i] operand being inverted. See also [constant LOGIC_OP_OR_REVERSE].

values.name: LOGIC_OP_OR_REVERSE
values.summary: OR logic operation with the [i]destination[/i] operand being inverted. See also [constant LOGIC_OP_OR_REVERSE].

values.name: LOGIC_OP_SET
values.summary: SET logic operation (result is always [code]1[/code]). See also [constant LOGIC_OP_CLEAR].

values.name: LOGIC_OP_XOR
values.summary: Exclusive or (XOR) logic operation.

values.name: MEMORY_BUFFERS
values.summary: Memory taken by buffers.

values.name: MEMORY_TEXTURES
values.summary: Memory taken by textures.

values.name: MEMORY_TOTAL
values.summary: Total memory taken. This is greater than the sum of [constant MEMORY_TEXTURES] and [constant MEMORY_BUFFERS], as it also includes miscellaneous memory usage.

values.name: DYNAMIC_STATE_DEPTH_BIAS
values.summary: Allows dynamically changing the depth bias.

values.name: DYNAMIC_STATE_LINE_WIDTH
values.summary: Allows dynamically changing the width of rendering lines.

values.name: PIPELINE_SPECIALIZATION_CONSTANT_TYPE_BOOL
values.summary: Boolean specialization constant.

values.name: PIPELINE_SPECIALIZATION_CONSTANT_TYPE_FLOAT
values.summary: Floating-point specialization constant.

values.name: PIPELINE_SPECIALIZATION_CONSTANT_TYPE_INT
values.summary: Integer specialization constant.

values.name: POLYGON_CULL_BACK
values.summary: Use polygon backface culling (faces pointing away from the camera are hidden).

values.name: POLYGON_CULL_DISABLED
values.summary: Do not use polygon front face or backface culling.

values.name: POLYGON_CULL_FRONT
values.summary: Use polygon frontface culling (faces pointing towards the camera are hidden).

values.name: POLYGON_FRONT_FACE_CLOCKWISE
values.summary: Clockwise winding order to determine which face of a polygon is its front face.

values.name: POLYGON_FRONT_FACE_COUNTER_CLOCKWISE
values.summary: Counter-clockwise winding order to determine which face of a polygon is its front face.

values.name: RENDER_PRIMITIVE_LINES
values.summary: Line list rendering primitive. Lines are drawn separated from each other.

values.name: RENDER_PRIMITIVE_LINESTRIPS
values.summary: Line strip rendering primitive. Lines drawn are connected to the previous vertex.

values.name: RENDER_PRIMITIVE_LINESTRIPS_WITH_ADJACENCY
values.summary: [url=https://registry.khronos.org/vulkan/specs/1.3-extensions/html/vkspec.html#drawing-line-strips-with-adjacency]Line strip rendering primitive with adjacency.[/url] [b]Note:[/b] Adjacency is only useful with geometry shaders, which Godot does not expose.

values.name: RENDER_PRIMITIVE_LINES_WITH_ADJACENCY
values.summary: [url=https://registry.khronos.org/vulkan/specs/1.3-extensions/html/vkspec.html#drawing-line-lists-with-adjacency]Line list rendering primitive with adjacency.[/url] [b]Note:[/b] Adjacency is only useful with geometry shaders, which Godot does not expose.

values.name: RENDER_PRIMITIVE_MAX
values.summary: Represents the size of the [enum RenderPrimitive] enum.

values.name: RENDER_PRIMITIVE_POINTS
values.summary: Point rendering primitive (with constant size, regardless of distance from camera).

values.name: RENDER_PRIMITIVE_TESSELATION_PATCH
values.summary: Tessellation patch rendering primitive. Only useful with tessellation shaders, which can be used to deform these patches.

values.name: RENDER_PRIMITIVE_TRIANGLES
values.summary: Triangle list rendering primitive. Triangles are drawn separated from each other.

values.name: RENDER_PRIMITIVE_TRIANGLES_WITH_ADJACENCY
values.summary: [url=https://registry.khronos.org/vulkan/specs/1.3-extensions/html/vkspec.html#drawing-triangle-lists-with-adjacency]Triangle list rendering primitive with adjacency.[/url] [b]Note:[/b] Adjacency is only useful with geometry shaders, which Godot does not expose.

values.name: RENDER_PRIMITIVE_TRIANGLE_STRIPS
values.summary: Triangle strip rendering primitive. Triangles drawn are connected to the previous triangle.

values.name: RENDER_PRIMITIVE_TRIANGLE_STRIPS_WITH_AJACENCY
values.summary: [url=https://registry.khronos.org/vulkan/specs/1.3-extensions/html/vkspec.html#drawing-triangle-strips-with-adjacency]Triangle strip rendering primitive with adjacency.[/url] [b]Note:[/b] Adjacency is only useful with geometry shaders, which Godot does not expose.

values.name: RENDER_PRIMITIVE_TRIANGLE_STRIPS_WITH_RESTART_INDEX
values.summary: Triangle strip rendering primitive with [i]primitive restart[/i] enabled. Triangles drawn are connected to the previous triangle, but a primitive restart index can be specified before drawing to create a second triangle strip after the specified index. [b]Note:[/b] Only compatible with indexed draws.

values.name: SAMPLER_BORDER_COLOR_FLOAT_OPAQUE_BLACK
values.summary: Return a floating-point opaque black color when sampling outside the [code][0.0, 1.0][/code] range. Only effective if the sampler repeat mode is [constant SAMPLER_REPEAT_MODE_CLAMP_TO_BORDER].

values.name: SAMPLER_BORDER_COLOR_FLOAT_OPAQUE_WHITE
values.summary: Return a floating-point opaque white color when sampling outside the [code][0.0, 1.0][/code] range. Only effective if the sampler repeat mode is [constant SAMPLER_REPEAT_MODE_CLAMP_TO_BORDER].

values.name: SAMPLER_BORDER_COLOR_FLOAT_TRANSPARENT_BLACK
values.summary: Return a floating-point transparent black color when sampling outside the [code][0.0, 1.0][/code] range. Only effective if the sampler repeat mode is [constant SAMPLER_REPEAT_MODE_CLAMP_TO_BORDER].

values.name: SAMPLER_BORDER_COLOR_INT_OPAQUE_BLACK
values.summary: Return an integer opaque black color when sampling outside the [code][0.0, 1.0][/code] range. Only effective if the sampler repeat mode is [constant SAMPLER_REPEAT_MODE_CLAMP_TO_BORDER].

values.name: SAMPLER_BORDER_COLOR_INT_OPAQUE_WHITE
values.summary: Return an integer opaque white color when sampling outside the [code][0.0, 1.0][/code] range. Only effective if the sampler repeat mode is [constant SAMPLER_REPEAT_MODE_CLAMP_TO_BORDER].

values.name: SAMPLER_BORDER_COLOR_INT_TRANSPARENT_BLACK
values.summary: Return an integer transparent black color when sampling outside the [code][0.0, 1.0][/code] range. Only effective if the sampler repeat mode is [constant SAMPLER_REPEAT_MODE_CLAMP_TO_BORDER].

values.name: SAMPLER_BORDER_COLOR_MAX
values.summary: Represents the size of the [enum SamplerBorderColor] enum.

values.name: SAMPLER_FILTER_LINEAR
values.summary: Bilinear sampler filtering. Sampling at higher resolutions than the source will result in a blurry look.

values.name: SAMPLER_FILTER_NEAREST
values.summary: Nearest-neighbor sampler filtering. Sampling at higher resolutions than the source will result in a pixelated look.

values.name: SAMPLER_REPEAT_MODE_CLAMP_TO_BORDER
values.summary: Sample with repeating disabled. When sampling outside the [code][0.0, 1.0][/code] range, return the specified [member RDSamplerState.border_color].

values.name: SAMPLER_REPEAT_MODE_CLAMP_TO_EDGE
values.summary: Sample with repeating disabled. When sampling outside the [code][0.0, 1.0][/code] range, return the color of the last pixel on the edge.

values.name: SAMPLER_REPEAT_MODE_MAX
values.summary: Represents the size of the [enum SamplerRepeatMode] enum.

values.name: SAMPLER_REPEAT_MODE_MIRRORED_REPEAT
values.summary: Sample with mirrored repeating enabled. When sampling outside the [code][0.0, 1.0][/code] range, return a mirrored version of the sampler. This mirrored version is mirrored again if sampling further away, with the pattern repeating indefinitely.

values.name: SAMPLER_REPEAT_MODE_MIRROR_CLAMP_TO_EDGE
values.summary: Sample with mirrored repeating enabled, but only once. When sampling in the [code][-1.0, 0.0][/code] range, return a mirrored version of the sampler. When sampling outside the [code][-1.0, 1.0][/code] range, return the color of the last pixel on the edge.

values.name: SAMPLER_REPEAT_MODE_REPEAT
values.summary: Sample with repeating enabled.

values.name: SHADER_LANGUAGE_GLSL
values.summary: Khronos' GLSL shading language (used natively by OpenGL and Vulkan). This is the language used for core Godot shaders.

values.name: SHADER_LANGUAGE_HLSL
values.summary: Microsoft's High-Level Shading Language (used natively by Direct3D, but can also be used in Vulkan).

values.name: SHADER_STAGE_ANY_HIT
values.summary: Any hit shader stage. Invoked when ray intersections are not opaque. This can be used to specify what happens when a ray hits any of the geometry in the scene.

values.name: SHADER_STAGE_ANY_HIT_BIT
values.summary: Any hit shader stage bit (see also [constant SHADER_STAGE_ANY_HIT]).

values.name: SHADER_STAGE_CLOSEST_HIT
values.summary: Closest hit shader stage. This can be used to specify what happens when a ray hits the closest geometry in the scene.

values.name: SHADER_STAGE_CLOSEST_HIT_BIT
values.summary: Closest hit shader stage bit (see also [constant SHADER_STAGE_CLOSEST_HIT]).

values.name: SHADER_STAGE_COMPUTE
values.summary: Compute shader stage. This can be used to run arbitrary computing tasks in a shader, performing them on the GPU instead of the CPU.

values.name: SHADER_STAGE_COMPUTE_BIT
values.summary: Compute shader stage bit (see also [constant SHADER_STAGE_COMPUTE]).

values.name: SHADER_STAGE_FRAGMENT
values.summary: Fragment shader stage (called "pixel shader" in Direct3D). This can be used to manipulate pixels from a shader.

values.name: SHADER_STAGE_FRAGMENT_BIT
values.summary: Fragment shader stage bit (see also [constant SHADER_STAGE_FRAGMENT]).

values.name: SHADER_STAGE_INTERSECTION
values.summary: Intersection shader stage. The intersection shader for triangles is built-in. This can be used to compute ray intersections with primitives that are not triangles.

values.name: SHADER_STAGE_INTERSECTION_BIT
values.summary: Intersection shader stage bit (see also [constant SHADER_STAGE_INTERSECTION]).

values.name: SHADER_STAGE_MAX
values.summary: Represents the size of the [enum ShaderStage] enum.

values.name: SHADER_STAGE_MISS
values.summary: Miss shader stage. This can be used to specify what happens if a ray does not hit anything in the scene.

values.name: SHADER_STAGE_MISS_BIT
values.summary: Miss shader stage bit (see also [constant SHADER_STAGE_MISS]).

values.name: SHADER_STAGE_RAYGEN
values.summary: Ray generation shader stage. This can be used to generate primary rays.

values.name: SHADER_STAGE_RAYGEN_BIT
values.summary: Ray generation shader stage bit (see also [constant SHADER_STAGE_RAYGEN]).

values.name: SHADER_STAGE_TESSELATION_CONTROL
values.summary: Tessellation control shader stage. This can be used to create additional geometry from a shader.

values.name: SHADER_STAGE_TESSELATION_CONTROL_BIT
values.summary: Tessellation control shader stage bit (see also [constant SHADER_STAGE_TESSELATION_CONTROL]).

values.name: SHADER_STAGE_TESSELATION_EVALUATION
values.summary: Tessellation evaluation shader stage. This can be used to create additional geometry from a shader.

values.name: SHADER_STAGE_TESSELATION_EVALUATION_BIT
values.summary: Tessellation evaluation shader stage bit (see also [constant SHADER_STAGE_TESSELATION_EVALUATION]).

values.name: SHADER_STAGE_VERTEX
values.summary: Vertex shader stage. This can be used to manipulate vertices from a shader (but not create new vertices).

values.name: SHADER_STAGE_VERTEX_BIT
values.summary: Vertex shader stage bit (see also [constant SHADER_STAGE_VERTEX]).

values.name: STENCIL_OP_DECREMENT_AND_CLAMP
values.summary: Decrement the existing stencil value and clamp to the minimum value if reached. Stencil bits are considered as an unsigned integer.

values.name: STENCIL_OP_DECREMENT_AND_WRAP
values.summary: Decrement the stencil value and wrap around to the maximum representable unsigned if reaching the minimum. Stencil bits are considered as an unsigned integer.

values.name: STENCIL_OP_INCREMENT_AND_CLAMP
values.summary: Increment the existing stencil value and clamp to the maximum representable unsigned value if reached. Stencil bits are considered as an unsigned integer.

values.name: STENCIL_OP_INCREMENT_AND_WRAP
values.summary: Increment the stencil value and wrap around to [code]0[/code] if reaching the maximum representable unsigned. Stencil bits are considered as an unsigned integer.

values.name: STENCIL_OP_INVERT
values.summary: Bitwise-invert the existing stencil value.

values.name: STENCIL_OP_KEEP
values.summary: Keep the current stencil value.

values.name: STENCIL_OP_MAX
values.summary: Represents the size of the [enum StencilOperation] enum.

values.name: STENCIL_OP_REPLACE
values.summary: Replace the existing stencil value with the new one.

values.name: STENCIL_OP_ZERO
values.summary: Set the stencil value to [code]0[/code].

values.name: TEXTURE_SAMPLES_1
values.summary: Perform 1 texture sample (this is the fastest but lowest-quality for antialiasing).

values.name: TEXTURE_SAMPLES_16
values.summary: Perform 16 texture samples. Not supported on mobile GPUs and many desktop GPUs.

values.name: TEXTURE_SAMPLES_2
values.summary: Perform 2 texture samples.

values.name: TEXTURE_SAMPLES_32
values.summary: Perform 32 texture samples. Not supported on most GPUs.

values.name: TEXTURE_SAMPLES_4
values.summary: Perform 4 texture samples.

values.name: TEXTURE_SAMPLES_64
values.summary: Perform 64 texture samples (this is the slowest but highest-quality for antialiasing). Not supported on most GPUs.

values.name: TEXTURE_SAMPLES_8
values.summary: Perform 8 texture samples. Not supported on mobile GPUs (including Apple Silicon).

values.name: TEXTURE_SAMPLES_MAX
values.summary: Represents the size of the [enum TextureSamples] enum.

values.name: TEXTURE_SLICE_2D
values.summary: 2-dimensional texture slice.

values.name: TEXTURE_SLICE_3D
values.summary: 3-dimensional texture slice.

values.name: TEXTURE_SLICE_CUBEMAP
values.summary: Cubemap texture slice.

values.name: TEXTURE_SWIZZLE_A
values.summary: Sample the alpha channel.

values.name: TEXTURE_SWIZZLE_B
values.summary: Sample the blue color channel.

values.name: TEXTURE_SWIZZLE_G
values.summary: Sample the green color channel.

values.name: TEXTURE_SWIZZLE_IDENTITY
values.summary: Return the sampled value as-is.

values.name: TEXTURE_SWIZZLE_MAX
values.summary: Represents the size of the [enum TextureSwizzle] enum.

values.name: TEXTURE_SWIZZLE_ONE
values.summary: Always return [code]1.0[/code] when sampling.

values.name: TEXTURE_SWIZZLE_R
values.summary: Sample the red color channel.

values.name: TEXTURE_SWIZZLE_ZERO
values.summary: Always return [code]0.0[/code] when sampling.

values.name: TEXTURE_TYPE_1D
values.summary: 1-dimensional texture.

values.name: TEXTURE_TYPE_1D_ARRAY
values.summary: Array of 1-dimensional textures.

values.name: TEXTURE_TYPE_2D
values.summary: 2-dimensional texture.

values.name: TEXTURE_TYPE_2D_ARRAY
values.summary: Array of 2-dimensional textures.

values.name: TEXTURE_TYPE_3D
values.summary: 3-dimensional texture.

values.name: TEXTURE_TYPE_CUBE
values.summary: [Cubemap] texture.

values.name: TEXTURE_TYPE_CUBE_ARRAY
values.summary: Array of [Cubemap] textures.

values.name: TEXTURE_TYPE_MAX
values.summary: Represents the size of the [enum TextureType] enum.

values.name: TEXTURE_USAGE_CAN_COPY_FROM_BIT
values.summary: Texture can be a source for [method texture_copy].

values.name: TEXTURE_USAGE_CAN_COPY_TO_BIT
values.summary: Texture can be a destination for [method texture_copy].

values.name: TEXTURE_USAGE_CAN_UPDATE_BIT
values.summary: Texture can be updated using [method texture_update].

values.name: TEXTURE_USAGE_COLOR_ATTACHMENT_BIT
values.summary: Texture can be used as a color attachment in a framebuffer.

values.name: TEXTURE_USAGE_CPU_READ_BIT
values.summary: Texture can be read back on the CPU using [method texture_get_data] faster than without this bit, since it is always kept in the system memory.

values.name: TEXTURE_USAGE_DEPTH_RESOLVE_ATTACHMENT_BIT
values.summary: Texture can be used as a depth/stencil resolve attachment in a framebuffer.

values.name: TEXTURE_USAGE_DEPTH_STENCIL_ATTACHMENT_BIT
values.summary: Texture can be used as a depth/stencil attachment in a framebuffer.

values.name: TEXTURE_USAGE_INPUT_ATTACHMENT_BIT
values.summary: Texture can be used as a [url=https://registry.khronos.org/vulkan/specs/1.3-extensions/html/vkspec.html#descriptorsets-inputattachment]input attachment[/url] in a framebuffer.

values.name: TEXTURE_USAGE_SAMPLING_BIT
values.summary: Texture can be sampled.

values.name: TEXTURE_USAGE_STORAGE_ATOMIC_BIT

constants.name: DEVICE_TYPE_OTHER
constants.summary: Rendering device type does not match any of the other enum values or is unknown.

constants.name: DEVICE_TYPE_INTEGRATED_GPU
constants.summary: Rendering device is an integrated GPU, which is typically [i](but not always)[/i] slower than dedicated GPUs ([constant DEVICE_TYPE_DISCRETE_GPU]). On Android and iOS, the rendering device type is always considered to be [constant DEVICE_TYPE_INTEGRATED_GPU].

constants.name: DEVICE_TYPE_DISCRETE_GPU
constants.summary: Rendering device is a dedicated GPU, which is typically [i](but not always)[/i] faster than integrated GPUs ([constant DEVICE_TYPE_INTEGRATED_GPU]).

constants.name: DEVICE_TYPE_VIRTUAL_GPU
constants.summary: Rendering device is an emulated GPU in a virtual environment. This is typically much slower than the host GPU, which means the expected performance level on a dedicated GPU will be roughly equivalent to [constant DEVICE_TYPE_INTEGRATED_GPU]. Virtual machine GPU passthrough (such as VFIO) will not report the device type as [constant DEVICE_TYPE_VIRTUAL_GPU]. Instead, the host GPU's device type will be reported as if the GPU was not emulated.

constants.name: DEVICE_TYPE_CPU
constants.summary: Rendering device is provided by software emulation (such as Lavapipe or [url=https://github.com/google/swiftshader]SwiftShader[/url]). This is the slowest kind of rendering device available; it's typically much slower than [constant DEVICE_TYPE_INTEGRATED_GPU].

constants.name: DEVICE_TYPE_MAX
constants.summary: Represents the size of the [enum DeviceType] enum.

constants.name: DRIVER_RESOURCE_LOGICAL_DEVICE
constants.summary: Specific device object based on a physical device ([code]rid[/code] parameter is ignored). - Vulkan: Vulkan device driver resource ([code]VkDevice[/code]). - D3D12: D3D12 device driver resource ([code]ID3D12Device[/code]). - Metal: Metal device driver resource ([code]MTLDevice[/code]).

constants.name: DRIVER_RESOURCE_PHYSICAL_DEVICE
constants.summary: Physical device the specific logical device is based on ([code]rid[/code] parameter is ignored). - Vulkan: [code]VkPhysicalDevice[/code]. - D3D12: [code]IDXGIAdapter[/code].

constants.name: DRIVER_RESOURCE_TOPMOST_OBJECT
constants.summary: Top-most graphics API entry object ([code]rid[/code] parameter is ignored). - Vulkan: [code]VkInstance[/code].

constants.name: DRIVER_RESOURCE_COMMAND_QUEUE

constants.name: DRIVER_RESOURCE_QUEUE_FAMILY
constants.summary: The specific family the main queue belongs to ([code]rid[/code] parameter is ignored). - Vulkan: The queue family index, a [code]uint32_t[/code].

constants.name: DRIVER_RESOURCE_TEXTURE_VIEW

constants.name: DRIVER_RESOURCE_TEXTURE_DATA_FORMAT

constants.name: DRIVER_RESOURCE_SAMPLER
constants.summary: - Vulkan: [code]VkSampler[/code].

constants.name: DRIVER_RESOURCE_BUFFER

constants.name: BARRIER_MASK_VERTEX
constants.summary: Vertex shader barrier mask.
