constants.summary: Fragment shader barrier mask.

constants.name: BARRIER_MASK_COMPUTE
constants.summary: Compute barrier mask.

constants.name: BARRIER_MASK_TRANSFER
constants.summary: Transfer barrier mask.

constants.name: BARRIER_MASK_RASTER
constants.summary: Raster barrier mask (vertex and fragment). Equivalent to [code]BARRIER_MASK_VERTEX | BARRIER_MASK_FRAGMENT[/code].

constants.name: BARRIER_MASK_ALL_BARRIERS
constants.summary: Barrier mask for all types (vertex, fragment, compute, transfer).

constants.name: BARRIER_MASK_NO_BARRIER
constants.summary: No barrier for any type.

constants.name: TEXTURE_TYPE_1D
constants.summary: 1-dimensional texture.

constants.name: TEXTURE_TYPE_2D
constants.summary: 2-dimensional texture.

constants.name: TEXTURE_TYPE_3D
constants.summary: 3-dimensional texture.

constants.name: TEXTURE_TYPE_CUBE
constants.summary: [Cubemap] texture.

constants.name: TEXTURE_TYPE_1D_ARRAY
constants.summary: Array of 1-dimensional textures.

constants.name: TEXTURE_TYPE_2D_ARRAY
constants.summary: Array of 2-dimensional textures.

constants.name: TEXTURE_TYPE_CUBE_ARRAY
constants.summary: Array of [Cubemap] textures.

constants.name: TEXTURE_TYPE_MAX
constants.summary: Represents the size of the [enum TextureType] enum.

constants.name: TEXTURE_SAMPLES_1
constants.summary: Perform 1 texture sample (this is the fastest but lowest-quality for antialiasing).

constants.name: TEXTURE_SAMPLES_2
constants.summary: Perform 2 texture samples.

constants.name: TEXTURE_SAMPLES_4
constants.summary: Perform 4 texture samples.

constants.name: TEXTURE_SAMPLES_8
constants.summary: Perform 8 texture samples. Not supported on mobile GPUs (including Apple Silicon).

constants.name: TEXTURE_SAMPLES_16
constants.summary: Perform 16 texture samples. Not supported on mobile GPUs and many desktop GPUs.

constants.name: TEXTURE_SAMPLES_32
constants.summary: Perform 32 texture samples. Not supported on most GPUs.

constants.name: TEXTURE_SAMPLES_64
constants.summary: Perform 64 texture samples (this is the slowest but highest-quality for antialiasing). Not supported on most GPUs.

constants.name: TEXTURE_SAMPLES_MAX
constants.summary: Represents the size of the [enum TextureSamples] enum.

constants.name: TEXTURE_USAGE_SAMPLING_BIT
constants.summary: Texture can be sampled.

constants.name: TEXTURE_USAGE_COLOR_ATTACHMENT_BIT
constants.summary: Texture can be used as a color attachment in a framebuffer.

constants.name: TEXTURE_USAGE_DEPTH_STENCIL_ATTACHMENT_BIT
constants.summary: Texture can be used as a depth/stencil attachment in a framebuffer.

constants.name: TEXTURE_USAGE_DEPTH_RESOLVE_ATTACHMENT_BIT
constants.summary: Texture can be used as a depth/stencil resolve attachment in a framebuffer.

constants.name: TEXTURE_USAGE_STORAGE_BIT
constants.summary: Texture can be used as a [url=https://registry.khronos.org/vulkan/specs/1.3-extensions/html/vkspec.html#descriptorsets-storageimage]storage image[/url].

constants.name: TEXTURE_USAGE_STORAGE_ATOMIC_BIT
constants.summary: Texture can be used as a [url=https://registry.khronos.org/vulkan/specs/1.3-extensions/html/vkspec.html#descriptorsets-storageimage]storage image[/url] with support for atomic operations.

constants.name: TEXTURE_USAGE_CPU_READ_BIT
constants.summary: Texture can be read back on the CPU using [method texture_get_data] faster than without this bit, since it is always kept in the system memory.

constants.name: TEXTURE_USAGE_CAN_UPDATE_BIT
constants.summary: Texture can be updated using [method texture_update].

constants.name: TEXTURE_USAGE_CAN_COPY_FROM_BIT
constants.summary: Texture can be a source for [method texture_copy].

constants.name: TEXTURE_USAGE_CAN_COPY_TO_BIT
constants.summary: Texture can be a destination for [method texture_copy].

constants.name: TEXTURE_USAGE_INPUT_ATTACHMENT_BIT
constants.summary: Texture can be used as a [url=https://registry.khronos.org/vulkan/specs/1.3-extensions/html/vkspec.html#descriptorsets-inputattachment]input attachment[/url] in a framebuffer.

constants.name: TEXTURE_SLICE_3D
constants.summary: 3-dimensional texture slice.

constants.name: SAMPLER_FILTER_NEAREST
constants.summary: Nearest-neighbor sampler filtering. Sampling at higher resolutions than the source will result in a pixelated look.

constants.name: SAMPLER_FILTER_LINEAR
constants.summary: Bilinear sampler filtering. Sampling at higher resolutions than the source will result in a blurry look.

constants.name: SAMPLER_REPEAT_MODE_REPEAT
constants.summary: Sample with repeating enabled.

constants.name: SAMPLER_REPEAT_MODE_MIRRORED_REPEAT
constants.summary: Sample with mirrored repeating enabled. When sampling outside the [code][0.0, 1.0][/code] range, return a mirrored version of the sampler. This mirrored version is mirrored again if sampling further away, with the pattern repeating indefinitely.

constants.name: SAMPLER_REPEAT_MODE_CLAMP_TO_EDGE
constants.summary: Sample with repeating disabled. When sampling outside the [code][0.0, 1.0][/code] range, return the color of the last pixel on the edge.

constants.name: SAMPLER_REPEAT_MODE_CLAMP_TO_BORDER
constants.summary: Sample with repeating disabled. When sampling outside the [code][0.0, 1.0][/code] range, return the specified [member RDSamplerState.border_color].

constants.name: SAMPLER_REPEAT_MODE_MIRROR_CLAMP_TO_EDGE
constants.summary: Sample with mirrored repeating enabled, but only once. When sampling in the [code][-1.0, 0.0][/code] range, return a mirrored version of the sampler. When sampling outside the [code][-1.0, 1.0][/code] range, return the color of the last pixel on the edge.

constants.name: SAMPLER_REPEAT_MODE_MAX
constants.summary: Represents the size of the [enum SamplerRepeatMode] enum.

constants.name: SAMPLER_BORDER_COLOR_FLOAT_TRANSPARENT_BLACK
constants.summary: Return a floating-point transparent black color when sampling outside the [code][0.0, 1.0][/code] range. Only effective if the sampler repeat mode is [constant SAMPLER_REPEAT_MODE_CLAMP_TO_BORDER].

constants.name: SAMPLER_BORDER_COLOR_INT_TRANSPARENT_BLACK
constants.summary: Return an integer transparent black color when sampling outside the [code][0.0, 1.0][/code] range. Only effective if the sampler repeat mode is [constant SAMPLER_REPEAT_MODE_CLAMP_TO_BORDER].

constants.name: SAMPLER_BORDER_COLOR_FLOAT_OPAQUE_BLACK
constants.summary: Return a floating-point opaque black color when sampling outside the [code][0.0, 1.0][/code] range. Only effective if the sampler repeat mode is [constant SAMPLER_REPEAT_MODE_CLAMP_TO_BORDER].

constants.name: SAMPLER_BORDER_COLOR_INT_OPAQUE_BLACK
constants.summary: Return an integer opaque black color when sampling outside the [code][0.0, 1.0][/code] range. Only effective if the sampler repeat mode is [constant SAMPLER_REPEAT_MODE_CLAMP_TO_BORDER].

constants.name: SAMPLER_BORDER_COLOR_FLOAT_OPAQUE_WHITE
constants.summary: Return a floating-point opaque white color when sampling outside the [code][0.0, 1.0][/code] range. Only effective if the sampler repeat mode is [constant SAMPLER_REPEAT_MODE_CLAMP_TO_BORDER].

constants.name: SAMPLER_BORDER_COLOR_INT_OPAQUE_WHITE
constants.summary: Return an integer opaque white color when sampling outside the [code][0.0, 1.0][/code] range. Only effective if the sampler repeat mode is [constant SAMPLER_REPEAT_MODE_CLAMP_TO_BORDER].

constants.name: SAMPLER_BORDER_COLOR_MAX
constants.summary: Represents the size of the [enum SamplerBorderColor] enum.

constants.name: VERTEX_FREQUENCY_VERTEX
constants.summary: Vertex attribute addressing is a function of the vertex. This is used to specify the rate at which vertex attributes are pulled from buffers.

constants.name: VERTEX_FREQUENCY_INSTANCE
constants.summary: Vertex attribute addressing is a function of the instance index. This is used to specify the rate at which vertex attributes are pulled from buffers.

constants.name: INDEX_BUFFER_FORMAT_UINT16
constants.summary: Index buffer in 16-bit unsigned integer format. This limits the maximum index that can be specified to [code]65535[/code].

constants.name: INDEX_BUFFER_FORMAT_UINT32
constants.summary: Index buffer in 32-bit unsigned integer format. This limits the maximum index that can be specified to [code]4294967295[/code].

constants.name: BUFFER_CREATION_DEVICE_ADDRESS_BIT
constants.summary: Optionally, set this flag if you wish to use [method buffer_get_device_address] functionality. You must first check the GPU supports it: [codeblocks] [gdscript] rd = RenderingServer.get_rendering_device() if rd.has_feature(RenderingDevice.SUPPORTS_BUFFER_DEVICE_ADDRESS): storage_buffer = rd.storage_buffer_create(bytes.size(), bytes, RenderingDevice.STORAGE_BUFFER_USAGE_SHADER_DEVICE_ADDRESS) storage_buffer_address = rd.buffer_get_device_address(storage_buffer) [/gdscript] [/codeblocks]

constants.name: BUFFER_CREATION_AS_STORAGE_BIT
constants.summary: Set this flag so that it is created as storage. This is useful if Compute Shaders need access (for reading or writing) to the buffer, e.g. skeletal animations are processed in Compute Shaders which need access to vertex buffers, to be later consumed by vertex shaders as part of the regular rasterization pipeline.

constants.name: BUFFER_CREATION_ACCELERATION_STRUCTURE_BUILD_INPUT_READ_ONLY_BIT
constants.summary: Allows usage of this buffer as input data for an acceleration structure build operation. You must first check that the GPU supports it: [codeblocks] [gdscript] rd = RenderingServer.get_rendering_device() if rd.has_feature(RenderingDevice.SUPPORTS_RAYTRACING_PIPELINE): storage_buffer = rd.storage_buffer_create(bytes.size(), bytes, RenderingDevice.BUFFER_CREATION_ACCELERATION_STRUCTURE_BUILD_INPUT_READ_ONLY_BIT) [/gdscript] [/codeblocks]

constants.name: ACCELERATION_STRUCTURE_ALLOW_UPDATE_BIT
constants.summary: Allows the acceleration structure to be updated after it has been built.

constants.name: ACCELERATION_STRUCTURE_ALLOW_COMPACTION_BIT
constants.summary: Allows the acceleration structure to be compacted to reduce memory usage after it has been built.

constants.name: ACCELERATION_STRUCTURE_PREFER_FAST_TRACE_BIT
constants.summary: Prioritizes ray traversal performance over build performance when building the acceleration structure.

constants.name: ACCELERATION_STRUCTURE_PREFER_FAST_BUILD_BIT

constants.name: ACCELERATION_STRUCTURE_INSTANCE_FORCE_NO_OPAQUE_BIT

constants.name: RENDER_PRIMITIVE_LINESTRIPS

constants.name: RENDER_PRIMITIVE_LINESTRIPS_WITH_ADJACENCY
constants.summary: [url=https://registry.khronos.org/vulkan/specs/1.3-extensions/html/vkspec.html#drawing-line-strips-with-adjacency]Line strip rendering primitive with adjacency.[/url] [b]Note:[/b] Adjacency is only useful with geometry shaders, which Godot does not expose.

constants.summary: Triangle list rendering primitive. Triangles are drawn separated from each other.

constants.name: RENDER_PRIMITIVE_TRIANGLES_WITH_ADJACENCY
constants.summary: [url=https://registry.khronos.org/vulkan/specs/1.3-extensions/html/vkspec.html#drawing-triangle-lists-with-adjacency]Triangle list rendering primitive with adjacency.[/url] [b]Note:[/b] Adjacency is only useful with geometry shaders, which Godot does not expose.

constants.name: RENDER_PRIMITIVE_TRIANGLE_STRIPS

constants.name: RENDER_PRIMITIVE_TRIANGLE_STRIPS_WITH_AJACENCY

constants.name: POLYGON_FRONT_FACE_COUNTER_CLOCKWISE

constants.name: STENCIL_OP_KEEP
constants.summary: Keep the current stencil value.

constants.name: STENCIL_OP_ZERO
constants.summary: Set the stencil value to [code]0[/code].

constants.name: STENCIL_OP_REPLACE
constants.summary: Replace the existing stencil value with the new one.

constants.name: STENCIL_OP_INCREMENT_AND_CLAMP
constants.summary: Increment the existing stencil value and clamp to the maximum representable unsigned value if reached. Stencil bits are considered as an unsigned integer.

constants.name: STENCIL_OP_DECREMENT_AND_CLAMP
constants.summary: Decrement the existing stencil value and clamp to the minimum value if reached. Stencil bits are considered as an unsigned integer.

constants.name: STENCIL_OP_INVERT
constants.summary: Bitwise-invert the existing stencil value.

constants.name: STENCIL_OP_INCREMENT_AND_WRAP
constants.summary: Increment the stencil value and wrap around to [code]0[/code] if reaching the maximum representable unsigned. Stencil bits are considered as an unsigned integer.

constants.name: STENCIL_OP_DECREMENT_AND_WRAP
constants.summary: Decrement the stencil value and wrap around to the maximum representable unsigned if reaching the minimum. Stencil bits are considered as an unsigned integer.

constants.name: STENCIL_OP_MAX
constants.summary: Represents the size of the [enum StencilOperation] enum.

constants.name: COMPARE_OP_GREATER_OR_EQUAL
constants.summary: "Greater than or equal" comparison.

constants.name: COMPARE_OP_MAX

constants.summary: Copy logic operation (keeps the [i]source[/i] value as-is). See also [constant LOGIC_OP_COPY_INVERTED] and [constant LOGIC_OP_NO_OP].

constants.name: LOGIC_OP_AND_INVERTED
constants.summary: AND logic operation with the [i]source[/i] operand being inverted. See also [constant LOGIC_OP_AND_REVERSE].

constants.name: LOGIC_OP_NO_OP
constants.summary: No-op logic operation (keeps the [i]destination[/i] value as-is). See also [constant LOGIC_OP_COPY].

constants.name: LOGIC_OP_XOR
constants.summary: Exclusive or (XOR) logic operation.

constants.name: LOGIC_OP_OR
constants.summary: OR logic operation.

constants.name: LOGIC_OP_EQUIVALENT
constants.summary: Not-XOR (XNOR) logic operation.

constants.name: LOGIC_OP_INVERT
constants.summary: Invert logic operation.

constants.summary: OR logic operation with the [i]destination[/i] operand being inverted. See also [constant LOGIC_OP_OR_REVERSE].

constants.summary: Color and alpha blend factor is [code]second source alpha[/code]. Only relevant for dual-source blending.

constants.summary: Color and alpha blend factor is [code]1.0 - second source alpha[/code]. Only relevant for dual-source blending.

constants.name: SHADER_STAGE_VERTEX

constants.summary: Fragment shader stage (called "pixel shader" in Direct3D). This can be used to manipulate pixels from a shader.

constants.name: SHADER_STAGE_TESSELATION_CONTROL
constants.summary: Tessellation control shader stage. This can be used to create additional geometry from a shader.

constants.name: SHADER_STAGE_TESSELATION_EVALUATION
constants.summary: Tessellation evaluation shader stage. This can be used to create additional geometry from a shader.

constants.name: SHADER_STAGE_COMPUTE
constants.summary: Compute shader stage. This can be used to run arbitrary computing tasks in a shader, performing them on the GPU instead of the CPU.

constants.name: SHADER_STAGE_RAYGEN
constants.summary: Ray generation shader stage. This can be used to generate primary rays.

constants.name: SHADER_STAGE_CLOSEST_HIT
constants.summary: Closest hit shader stage. This can be used to specify what happens when a ray hits the closest geometry in the scene.

constants.name: SHADER_STAGE_MISS
constants.summary: Miss shader stage. This can be used to specify what happens if a ray does not hit anything in the scene.

constants.name: SHADER_STAGE_INTERSECTION
constants.summary: Intersection shader stage. The intersection shader for triangles is built-in. This can be used to compute ray intersections with primitives that are not triangles.

constants.summary: Tessellation control shader stage bit (see also [constant SHADER_STAGE_TESSELATION_CONTROL]).

constants.summary: Tessellation evaluation shader stage bit (see also [constant SHADER_STAGE_TESSELATION_EVALUATION]).

constants.name: SHADER_STAGE_COMPUTE_BIT
constants.summary: Compute shader stage bit (see also [constant SHADER_STAGE_COMPUTE]).

constants.name: SHADER_STAGE_RAYGEN_BIT
constants.summary: Ray generation shader stage bit (see also [constant SHADER_STAGE_RAYGEN]).

constants.summary: Any hit shader stage bit (see also [constant SHADER_STAGE_ANY_HIT]).

constants.name: SHADER_STAGE_CLOSEST_HIT_BIT
constants.summary: Closest hit shader stage bit (see also [constant SHADER_STAGE_CLOSEST_HIT]).

constants.summary: Miss shader stage bit (see also [constant SHADER_STAGE_MISS]).
